title: Homer M. Hadley Memorial Bridge - Wikipedia
content: | The two spans of the Homer M. Hadley Memorial Bridge (left) and the Lacey V. Hadley Memorial Bridge**, also known as the **Third Lake Washington Bridge**, is a floating bridge in the Seattle metropolitan area of the U.S. state of Washington "Washington (state)"). It is one of the Interstate 90 floating bridges, carrying the westbound lanes of Interstate 90 and two tracks of the 2 Line "2 Line (Sound Transit)"), a Link light rail line, across Lake Washington between Mercer Island and Seattle. A third floating bridge on Lake Washington was proposed in the 1950s during construction of the Evergreen Point Floating Bridge "Evergreen Point Floating Bridge (1963)") to the north. Sound Transit and the Washington State Department of Transportation added HOV lanes to the bridge's westbound lanes in 2017. With a total of five traffic lanes and three full-sized shoulders "Shoulder (road)"), the Third Lake Washington Bridge was the widest floating bridge in the world, until the completion of the new Evergreen Point Floating Bridge "Evergreen Point Floating Bridge (2016)") in 2016.

title: Keeping watch on a one-of-a-kind bridge
content: The I-90 Homer M. Hadley Memorial Bridge is, in a sense, a boat. Its concrete pontoons float on Lake Washington between Seattle and Mercer

title: Homer M. Hadley Memorial Bridge - Washington - SmartGuide
content: The Homer M. Hadley Memorial Bridge spans 1.3 miles (2.1 kilometers) across Lake Washington, securing it a spot among the longest floating bridges globally.
